fields.label: Origin
fields.value: Formed near the Great Aetheric Rift

lead:
**Aether-Crystals** are rare mineral formations suffused with primordial magical energy, occurring naturally in proximity to the [[Great Aetheric Rift]]. They are the single most important resource in [[Aethelgard]], serving as the foundation of both arcane practice and industrial technology.

Unlike conventional gems or minerals, Aether-Crystals are not merely inert objects: they actively interact with living creatures, ambient magical fields, and mechanical systems. When properly tuned, a single Grade-A crystal can power a [[Steam-Golems|Steam-Golem]] for three weeks or illuminate an entire city block indefinitely. Control over Aether-Crystal deposits is the primary driver of political conflict between the [[Lumina Collective]] and the [[Ironclad Dominion]].

content.content:
Aether-Crystals form over centuries in deposits of a particular mineral matrix — geologists call it **aethite** — when exposed to sustained aetheric radiation from the [[Great Aetheric Rift]]. The deeper the deposit, the higher the crystal's energy density and the darker its characteristic blue-violet colour.

The largest known deposits are located in the **Northern Rift Basin**, where the [[Lumina Collective]] maintains exclusive extraction rights. Secondary deposits exist throughout the Central Plains, though these are contested fiercely by the [[Ironclad Dominion]].

content.caption: Natural cave formation of Aether-Crystals in the Northern Rift Basin

content.title: Extraction Hazards
content.content: Improper extraction of Aether-Crystals can trigger an aetheric discharge capable of causing severe burns, temporary blindness, and in extreme cases, spontaneous crystallisation of surrounding rock for up to 200 metres. All extraction operations must be supervised by a certified Resonance Engineer.

The **Aethelgard Crystal Grading Standard** (ACGS), established by the [[Lumina Collective]] in 1702 AE, classifies crystals on a six-tier scale from Grade-A (raw, maximum potency) to Grade-F (fully depleted, no measurable aetheric output).

rows.Primary Use: High arcane workings, city power grids

content.content:
The range of applications for Aether-Crystals is vast. In arcane contexts, they serve as amplifiers for magical workings, communication arrays, and the famous **Crystal-Song** healing technique practised by [[Life-Threads]] weavers.

In industrial contexts, they power [[Steam-Golems]], drive the aetheric turbines of major cities, and serve as the navigational cores of [[Cloud-Skimmers]]. The [[Sky-Merchants Guild]] estimates that over 60% of their trade volume consists of Aether-Crystals in various grades.

items.caption: The crystal-lit skyline of Aethelheim at night
Technicians installing a Grade-B crystal into a Steam-Golem core
A long-range crystal communication array in the Northern Reaches

content.content:
Control over Aether-Crystal deposits has been the central driver of geopolitical conflict in [[Aethelgard]] since the Age of Factions. The [[Lumina Collective]]'s argument that crystals should be managed as a shared resource conflicts directly with the [[Ironclad Dominion]]'s view of them as strategic assets to be secured and monopolised.

The **Crystal Accords** (1801 AE) established a nominal international framework for crystal extraction rights, but their enforcement mechanisms remain weak and frequently contested.
He who holds the crystals holds the future. Everything else is negotiation.
content.author: High Marshal Torchyn, Ironclad Dominion, 1789 AE

references.title: The Crystal Accords: Text and Commentary

editHistory.summary: Added extraction hazards callout, expanded formation section
